reviews.review_msg: PLEASE remind your team about the evaluation policy. Evaluated

Legendary Prof! Christian explains things clearly, gives useful feedback, and creates an environment where you can improve a lot if you put in the effort. I would strongly suggest that you start assignments early and GO TO OFFICE HOURS. Anyone who says this course is hard has procrastinated their life away when they should've just put in the work.

Christian is the GOAT. His class was one of the few I looked forward to attending because I actually felt like I was learning something. Begin your assignments early, and ATTEND OFFICE HOURS. He has a lot of valuable feedback to offer. My only regret is not going to more of them.

very biased prof who demands work be exactly how he sees fit. tough marker for no reason. unreasonable expectations. work he expects is nothing like the real world. he needs to ease up. penalizes you for not attending optional office hours as well.

Bro is a elite prof that balances his history as a caseit coach alongside the 360w content 

His content is relevant to the industry without being impractical and ignore the comments him being too harsh he just keeps it real and wants to improve ur grasp of the English language and business vocab so u rep beedie school of business well in his eyes

Most of your grade depends on revisions and improvements (and this course isn't curved). Christian will provide you with very detailed feedback; if you meet with him or TAs via office hours, you will be very likely to succeed. I recommend putting in the effort to improve your communication skills through this course- it'll pay off in the long run.

Christian seems like a nice guy tbh. If you go to his office hours he will provide you with good feedback on how to improve your writing. Unfortunately, 40% of your mark is from the group project (outline+ppt+presentation) so it's difficult to get a good mark, especially since the class isn't curved.

He is super difficult but you will learn a lot. I was super stressed during the semester and was praying for his downfall, but looking back, I gained a lot from his high expectations. Just be ready to spend a lot of time on assignments and don't worry too much about individual grades, he notices improvement. Just go to office hours it helps

Christian is an amazing professor who is really serious about the material. Participation really matters in this class. However Christian is a really tough grader and if you don't have proficient writing skills then you should attend his office hours more often to get more feedback otherwise you will most likely struggle in this class.
